description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Kentucky (US:  , UK: ), officially the Commonwealth of Kentucky, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Maryland (US:  ) is a state in the Mid-Atlantic and Southeastern regions of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: city in Ohio, United States; county seat of Hamilton County
description_wiki: Cincinnati (  SIN-sih-NAT-ee; colloquially nicknamed Cincy) is the most populous city in Hamilton County, Ohio, United States, and its county seat.

description: city in and county seat of Fayette County, Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Lexington is a consolidated city coterminous with Fayette County, Kentucky, United States, of which it is also the county seat.

description: public university in the United States
description_wiki: The University of Kentucky (UK, UKY, or U of K) is a public land-grant research university in Lexington, Kentucky, United States.

description: social movement originating in the USA
description_wiki: Black Lives Matter (BLM) is a decentralized political and social movement that aims to highlight racism, discrimination and racial inequality experienced by Black people in the United States, and to promote anti-racism.

description: loose-knit group of independent collectives, serving free vegan and vegetarian food to others
description_wiki: Food Not Bombs (FNB) is a loose-knit group of independent collectives that distribute free, usually vegan and vegetarian food.

surface_forms: one of these Reclaim the Streets events
description: resistance movement aiming for community ownership of public spaces
description_wiki: Reclaim the Streets, also known as RTS, are a collective with a shared ideal of community ownership of public spaces.
Kentuckians for the Commonwealth (KFTC) is a grassroots community organization under the original name of the Kentuckian Fair Tax Coalition.
